hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Returns error string: 'error=invalid_bucket_increment_count' when the increment count in the input does not match the number of defined buckets plus one (inclusive of +Inf).
Returns error string: 'error=metric_family_type_conflict' if a metric family with a duplicate name is attempted or if a new metric type conflicts with an existing family name.
Returns error string: 'error=invalid_base64' if the encoded string contains non-base64 characters OR if it is valid base64 followed by too many '=' padding characters or trailing empty characters.

context_pointers.actual_meaning: The code path that iterates through a list of metric family/type definitions and adds them to the global registry object.

context_pointers.vague_reference: same metric family serialization format used in exporters.go
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The specific JSON or string serialization format for individual metric instances within a family, likely pointing to a template or helper function string.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_http_metrics_endpoint.json.description: An HTTP endpoint exposes registered metric registries at configured paths with status, content-type, optional compression, authentication, removal, and expired-registry behavior observable through requests.

cases.expected_output:
request_path=/first
status=200
content_type=text/plain; charset=utf-8
contains_first_total=yes
contains_second_total=no
request_path=/second
status=200
content_type=text/plain; charset=utf-8
contains_second_total=yes
contains_first_total=no

request_path=/metrics
status=200
content_type=text/plain; charset=utf-8
contains_first_total=yes
contains_second_total=no

feature1_counter_accumulation.json.description: Counter samples expose the accumulated non-negative increment total after a sequence of observations.

feature2_gauge_value_updates.json.description: Gauge samples expose the latest numeric value after increments, decrements, and direct assignments.

feature3_histogram_buckets.json.description: Histogram samples expose total count, total sum, and cumulative bucket counts for configured upper bounds plus an infinity bucket.

cases.expected_output:
sample_count=0
sample_sum=0
bucket_le=1 count=0
bucket_le=2 count=0
bucket_le=+Inf count=0

sample_count=7
sample_sum=9.5
bucket_le=1 count=3
bucket_le=2 count=6
bucket_le=+Inf count=7

sample_count=43
sample_sum=54
bucket_le=1 count=5
bucket_le=2 count=34
bucket_le=+Inf count=43

sample_count=1
sample_sum=-10
bucket_le=1 count=1
bucket_le=+Inf count=1

sample_count=2
sample_sum=3
bucket_le=1 count=0
bucket_le=2 count=2
bucket_le=+Inf count=2

feature4_summary_quantiles.json.description: Summary samples expose total count, total sum, and configured quantile estimates after observations.

feature5_name_validation.json.description: Metric and label names are accepted only when they are non-empty, unreserved, and use the permitted identifier characters.

input.name: fa mi ly with space in name or |

cases.expected_output:
kind=metric
name=fa mi ly with space in name or |
valid=no

feature6_metric_family_membership.json.description: A metric family combines constant and per-sample labels, reports collected members, supports removal, and rejects conflicting label keys.

cases.expected_output:
family_count=1
name=total_requests
help=Counts all requests
metric_count=1
metric=0
component=test
status=200
counter_value=0

family_count=1
name=total_requests
help=Counts all requests
metric_count=1
metric=0
counter_value=1

family_count=1
name=total_requests
help=Counts all requests
metric_count=1
metric=0
name=counter2
counter_value=0

feature7_registry_collection_rules.json.description: A registry exposes registered metric families, removes them when requested, merges matching families in merge mode, and rejects name/type conflicts.

cases.expected_output:
family_count=1
name=test
help=a test
metric_count=2
name=counter2
name=counter1

feature8_text_exposition_format.json.description: Metric families serialize to the text exposition format with HELP/TYPE headers, escaped label values, special floating-point values, timestamps, and compound sample lines.

cases.expected_output:
# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric gauge
my_metric Nan

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric gauge
my_metric -Inf

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric gauge
my_metric +Inf

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric gauge
my_metric{k="v\\v"} 0

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric gauge
my_metric{k="v\nv"} 0

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric gauge
my_metric{k="v\"v"} 0

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric untyped
my_metric 64

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric counter
my_metric 64 1234

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric histogram
my_metric_count 2
my_metric_sum 200
my_metric_bucket{le="1"} 1
my_metric_bucket{le="+Inf"} 2

# HELP my_metric my metric help text
# TYPE my_metric summary
my_metric_count 2
my_metric_sum 200
my_metric{quantile="0.5"} 0

feature9_base64_decoding.json.description: Base64 encoded input decodes to its byte string, while malformed alphabet, size, or padding input yields a neutral invalid-input error.

_constraints.output_rule: increment total by 'value' if positive, otherwise ignore (do not subtract)
increment total sample count by 1, add value to sum, increment count for all buckets <= value
check increment count matches number of buckets + 1 (+Inf). If mismatch, return error.
include in sample count and sum, increment +Inf bucket only if value <= bucket bound (always true for +Inf), increment all buckets where value <= bucket bound
return count and sum, no quantile lines

_constraints.input_condition: kind is 'metric' and name is non-empty
name contains whitespace or invalid characters (non-alphanumeric, underscores, digits)
kind is 'label' and name is all uppercase

_constraints.output_rule: serialize each metric instance. If metric data is empty or null, output empty string for that line.
increment the value of the metric
exclude those metrics from the final output
return error if a metric family with duplicate name exists or new metric type conflicts
merge by name and sum values (not describe merging logic, only result structure)

_constraints.input_condition: registers a new metric family or type

_constraints.output_rule: return decoded string without trailing newline unless embedded newline matters (test case C005 shows newline stripped for valid inputs)

_constraints.input_condition: encoded string is base64 followed by too many '=' or empty chars
path does not match any registry path
registry path matches request path (e.g. /metrics or /specific)

hide.oracle_response: Returns error string: 'error=invalid_bucket_increment_count' when the increment count in the input does not match the number of defined buckets plus one (inclusive of +Inf).
Returns error string: 'error=metric_family_type_conflict' if a metric family with a duplicate name is attempted or if a new metric type conflicts with an existing family name.

hide.replacement: padding or padding plus empty characters error
hide.oracle_response: Returns error string: 'error=invalid_base64' if the encoded string contains non-base64 characters OR if it is valid base64 followed by too many '=' padding characters or trailing empty characters.
